items.text: FORK A (CI shape) resolved + logged: GitHub Actions workflow running 'python -m pytest -q' on push + PR, the python-version matrix (floor justified by syntax: future-annotations everywhere, no match -> propose 3.10+ and let the matrix prove it), and that 'required check' is the operator's branch-protection setting (not something the agent toggles).

FORK B (version/tag consistency) resolved + logged: a check that the release git tag equals plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json version, and WHERE it runs (CI release job and/or a dogfood DoD command-check).
FORK C (release process) resolved + logged: minimal tag -> GitHub release flow; tag/source as the pin (a built archive optional).

PYPROJECT (operator decision, overrides iteration-010 refinement): a pyproject.toml IS added. Decision + plan must fix (i) its purpose - package metadata + dev/tooling (editable install, pytest config), the marketplace plugin remains the install path, NOT PyPI as the primary channel; (ii) the SINGLE-SOURCE guard - plugin.json stays the version of record and a consistency check fails CI if pyproject's version diverges (or pyproject reads it / is generated), so there is never a second source of truth; (iii) the src-layout mapping (plugin/src/lume).

FORK D (install verification) resolved + logged: how to close the [U] live-install caveat - a documented, reproduced manual verification vs an automated job - with the limitation (Claude CLI in CI) stated.

FORK E (scope guard) restated: delivery only. The iteration-010 'no pyproject' refinement is OVERRIDDEN by operator decision (pyproject added); the guard now is that pyproject must not duplicate the version of record (plugin.json) and PyPI is not introduced as the primary install path.

Build plan recorded via lume plan add: ordered execution (+closeout) iterations - e.g. pyproject + version-consistency check, CI workflow, tag+release process, install-verification doc - each a binary DoD sketch + committed/optional tag; the first is concrete enough to open next.
Scope held: no engine/CI code in this planning iteration.

dod.preamble: P5/L4 planning: turn the delivery forks from iteration 010 (CI, version/tag consistency, release process, install verification, scope guard) into logged decisions and an ordered build plan. OPERATOR DECISION: a pyproject.toml IS in scope (overrides the iteration-010 'no pyproject' refinement); plugin.json stays the single version of record and a consistency check guards against drift - PyPI is not the primary channel. Decisions + plan only; no CI or engine code - building is the execution iterations.

handback: P5/L4 planning done: five forks resolved as logged decisions + a 4-step build plan (P11-P14, committed). DECISIONS: (A) CI = GitHub Actions 'pytest -q' on push/PR, matrix 3.10-3.13; required-check is the operator's branch-protection setting. (PYPROJECT, operator override of iter-010) add pyproject.toml for metadata+tooling over src-layout; plugin.json stays the SINGLE version of record and a consistency check fails CI on drift; PyPI is not the primary channel. (B) version = tag vX.Y.Z == plugin.json version (and == pyproject); (C) release = tag + GitHub release, tag/ref is the pin, archive optional; (D) close the [U] live-install caveat with a documented reproduced manual verification (Claude-CLI-in-CI is out of v0.1). PLAN: P11 pyproject+consistency check, P12 CI workflow, P13 tag+release process, P14 install-verification doc + closeout. P11 is concrete enough to open next (touches pyproject.toml + a consistency test). No CI/engine code this iteration. RECOMMEND next: open the P11 execution iteration.
self_review: Holds: each fork is closed with a decision that fits how lume actually ships (marketplace plugin, stdlib-only) and the operator's pyproject request is honoured WITH the single-source guard that was the whole risk - a CI-enforced consistency check, not a trust-the-human sync. The plan isolates the version-source change (P11) from CI (P12) so the consistency check exists before CI depends on it. Weak / watch: (1) CONSISTENCY CHECK READING pyproject - asserting pyproject.version == plugin.json needs a TOML parse; python 3.11+ has tomllib but the 3.10 matrix leg does not, so the checker must either vendor a tiny parser, depend on tomli for 3.10, or the floor moves to 3.11 - an execution-time decision I flagged now so it is not a surprise. (2) Several true completions are OPERATOR actions, not agent ones: making CI a required check, and cutting the actual v0.1.0 tag/release; the plan marks these as noted-not-done, so P5's done-when will be 'met pending two operator actions' rather than fully closed by the agent - called out so the closeout is honest. (3) The live-install verification is manual/documented, weaker than an automated gate; accepted as the v0.1 close per the logged decision. Nothing deferred silently.
